'''Morgan Smith''' (born 30 April 1998) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh|so}} or {{rlp|hk}} for [[Oldham RLFC|Oldham]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

He previously played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] and [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] at the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]]. Smith has also played for the [[London Broncos]] in the Super League and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] as well as the [[York City Knights]] and [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship]].

==Background==
Morgan Smith is the grandson of the rugby league footballer; [[Peter Smith (rugby league, born 1955)|Peter Smith]].<ref name="Barrie McDermott picks a team of young players that will make an impact in Super League 2015">{{cite web|url=http://www.skysports.com/rugby-league/news/12196/9656099/barrie-mcdermott-picks-a-team-of-youth-players-that-will-make-an-impact-in-super-league-2015|title=Barrie McDermott picks a team of young players that will make an impact in Super League 2015|publisher=Sky Sports|date=2015-01-22|accessdate=2016-09-13}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Warrington Wolves===
He has played for the [[Warrington Wolves]] in the [[Super League]], and on loan from [[Warrington Wolves|Warrington]] for the [[Rochdale Hornets]] in the [[2018 Betfred Championship|Betfred Championship]].<ref name="Profile at loverugbyleague.com"/><ref name="Statistics at rugbyleagueproject.org"/> Smith has also played for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

===Featherstone Rovers===
On 21 Oct 2021 it was reported that he had signed for [[Featherstone Rovers]] in the [[RFL Championship]].<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=21 Oct 2021|accessdate=26 Dec 2021|title=Morgan Smith over the moon to join his hometown club|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/york-star-morgan-smith-joins-featherstone/}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity===
On 7 October 2022 it was reported that he had signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=7 Oct 2022|accessdate=7 Oct 2022|title=Morgan Smith: Wakefield Trinity sign Featherstone Rovers half-back|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63171099}}</ref>

===Hull FC===
On 6 October 2023 it was reported that he had signed for [[Hull F.C.]] on a two-year deal. Smith made 25 appearances for Hull F.C. in the 2024 Super League season as the club finished 11th on the table.<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=6 Oct 2023|accessdate=8 Oct 2023|title=Morgan Smith: Hull FC sign Wakefield Trinity half-back on two-year deal from 2024|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/67033230}}</ref><ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/hull-fc-sit-bottom-img-9630900/|title=Hull FC sit bottom of IMG performance but KR surge up as full Super League picture laid out|website=www.hulldailymail.co.uk}}</ref>

===Sheffield Eagles===
On 22 November 2024, it was reported that he had signed for [[Sheffield Eagles|Sheffield]] in the RFL Championship on a two-year deal.<ref>{{cite web|website=Total RL|date=22 Nov 2024|author=Callum Walker|title=Ex-Hull FC playmaker Morgan Smith finds new club|url=https://www.totalrl.com/ex-hull-fc-playmaker-morgan-smith-finds-new-club/}}</ref>

===Oldham RLFC===
On 11 July 2025 it was reported that he had signed for [[Oldham RLFC]] in the [[RFL Championship]]<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=11 Jul 2025|author=Louis Chapman Coombe|title=Former Super League duo in Championship swap move ahead of unique fixture|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/former-super-league-duo-in-championship-swap-move-ahead-of-unique-fixture}}</ref>
